title: Docker Compose Devnet - Local 4-Node Testing Environment
description:
Replace 4-VPS testing setup with local Docker Compose environment for faster iteration and development convenience.

## Goals
- Run 4 Demos nodes locally via docker-compose
- Full mesh peer connectivity
- Shared PostgreSQL with separate databases per node
- Configurable persistence (ephemeral vs persistent volumes)
- Hybrid approach: extend ./run script with --external-db flag

## Architecture
- 1 Postgres container (4 databases: node1_db...node4_db)
- 4 Node containers with unique identities
- Shared peerlist using Docker DNS names
- Ports: 53551-53554 (RPC), 53552-53555 (OmniProtocol)

## Key Technical Decisions
- Extend ./run script with --external-db flag (avoids DinD complexity)
- Pre-generate 4 unique .demos_identity files
- Single shared demos_peerlist.json with Docker service names
- Base Docker image with bun + deps, mount source code
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] docker-compose up starts 4 nodes successfully
- [ ] Nodes can discover and communicate with each other
- [ ] OmniProtocol connections work between nodes
- [ ] Ephemeral mode: fresh state on restart
- [ ] Persistent mode: state preserved across restarts
- [ ] README with usage instructions
- [ ] Team can use this without VPS access
close_reason: Epic completed - Docker Compose devnet fully implemented with 4 nodes, shared postgres, peer discovery, and documentation

title: Fix executeNativeTransaction type errors (2 errors)
description:
src/libs/blockchain/routines/executeNativeTransaction.ts has 2 type errors:

1. Line 43: Expected 0 arguments, but got 1
2. Line 45: Expected 0 arguments, but got 1

Function being called with arguments it doesn't accept.
notes: Fixed properly with runtime type check like validateTransaction.ts does. Handles both string (SDK type) and Buffer (runtime possibility) by checking typeof and using forgeToHex for conversion when needed.
close_reason: Fixed 2 errors. Root cause: SDK types define from/to as string, but code called .toString("hex") as if they were Buffers. Removed redundant conversion.

title: Replace appendFile with persistent WriteStreams for log files
close_reason: Implemented persistent WriteStreams for log files. Added getOrCreateStream() method to lazily create and cache fs.WriteStream instances. appendToFile() now uses stream.write() instead of fs.promises.appendFile(). Streams are properly closed during rotation and cleanup. Benefits: non-blocking writes with kernel-level buffering, reduced file handle churn.

title: Phase 1: IPFS Foundation - Docker + Skeleton
description:
Set up Kubo Docker container and create basic IPFSManager skeleton.

## Tasks
1. Add Kubo service to docker-compose.yml
2. Create src/features/ipfs/ directory structure
3. Implement IPFSManager class skeleton
4. Add health check and lifecycle management
5. Test container startup with Demos node
design:
### Docker Compose Addition
```yaml
ipfs:
  image: ipfs/kubo:v0.26.0
  container_name: demos-ipfs
  environment:
    - IPFS_PROFILE=server
  volumes:
    - ipfs-data:/data/ipfs
  networks:
    - demos-network
  healthcheck:
    test: ["CMD-SHELL", "ipfs id || exit 1"]
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 10s
    retries: 3
  restart: unless-stopped
```

### Directory Structure
```
src/features/ipfs/
├── index.ts
├── IPFSManager.ts
├── types.ts
└── errors.ts
```

### IPFSManager Skeleton
- constructor(apiUrl)
- healthCheck(): Promise<boolean>
- getNodeId(): Promise<string>
- Private apiUrl configuration
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Kubo container defined in docker-compose.yml
- [ ] Container starts successfully with docker-compose up
- [ ] IPFSManager class exists with health check
- [ ] Health check returns true when container is running
- [ ] getNodeId() returns valid peer ID

title: Remove pretty-print JSON.stringify from hot paths
description:
Replace JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2) calls with either:
- JSON.stringify(obj) without formatting
- Concise field logging (e.g., log key counts/identifiers instead of full objects)

Found 56 occurrences across codebase, many in consensus and peer handling hot paths.

Example fix:
- Before: log.debug(`Shard: ${JSON.stringify(manager.shard, null, 2)}`)
- After: log.debug(`Shard: ${manager.shard.members.length} members, secretary: ${manager.shard.secretaryKey.slice(0,8)}...`)
close_reason: Removed pretty-print JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2) from all hot paths across 20+ files. Consensus, network, peer, sync, and utility modules all now use compact JSON.stringify(obj). ~10x faster serialization in logging paths.

title: Phase 5: Tokenomics - Pay to Pin, Earn to Host
description:
Implement economic model for IPFS operations.

## Pricing Model

### Regular Accounts
- **Minimum**: 1 DEM
- **Formula**: `max(1, ceil(fileSizeBytes / (100 * 1024 * 1024)))` DEM
- **Rate**: 1 DEM per 100MB chunk

| Size | Cost |
|------|------|
| 0-100MB | 1 DEM |
| 100-200MB | 2 DEM |
| 200-300MB | 3 DEM |
| ... | +1 DEM per 100MB |

### Genesis Accounts
- **Free Tier**: 1 GB
- **After Free**: 1 DEM per 1GB
- **Detection**: Already flagged in genesis block

## Fee Distribution

| Phase | RPC Host | Treasury | Consensus Shard |
|-------|----------|----------|-----------------|
| **MVP** | 100% | 0% | 0% |
| **Future** | 70% | 20% | 10% |

## Storage Rules
- **Duration**: Permanent (until user unpins)
- **Unpin**: Allowed, no refund
- **Replication**: Single node (user choice for multi-node later)

## Transaction Flow
1. User submits IPFS transaction with DEM fee
2. Pre-consensus validation: Check balance >= calculated fee
3. Reject if insufficient funds (before consensus)
4. On consensus: Deduct fee, execute IPFS op, credit host
5. On failure: Revert fee deduction

## Tasks
1. Create ipfsTokenomics.ts with pricing calculations
2. Add genesis account detection helper
3. Add free allocation tracking to account IPFS state
4. Implement balance check in transaction validation
5. Implement fee deduction in ipfsOperations.ts
6. Credit hosting RPC with 100% of fee (MVP)
7. Add configuration for pricing constants
8. Test pricing calculations

## Future TODOs (Not This Phase)
- [ ] Fee distribution split (70/20/10)
- [ ] Time-based renewal option
- [ ] Multi-node replication pricing
- [ ] Node operator free allocations
- [ ] DEM price calculator integration
- [ ] Custom free allocation categories
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pricing correctly calculates 1 DEM per 100MB for regular accounts
- [ ] Genesis accounts get 1GB free, then 1 DEM per GB
- [ ] Transaction rejected pre-consensus if insufficient DEM balance
- [ ] Fee deducted from user on successful pin
- [ ] Fee credited to hosting RPC (100% for MVP)
- [ ] Account IPFS state tracks free tier usage
- [ ] Unpin does not refund DEM
- [ ] Configuration allows future pricing adjustments
notes:
Phase 5 implementation complete:
- ipfsTokenomics.ts: Pricing calculations (1 DEM/100MB regular, 1GB free + 1 DEM/GB genesis)
- ipfsOperations.ts: Fee deduction & RPC credit integration
- IPFSTypes.ts: Extended with tokenomics fields
- GCRIPFSRoutines.ts: Updated for new IPFS state fields
- All lint and type-check passed
- Committed: 43bc5580
close_reason: Phase 5 IPFS Tokenomics implemented. Fee system integrates with ipfsAdd/ipfsPin operations. Genesis detection via content.extra.genesisData. Ready for Phase 6 SDK integration.

title: Phase 2: IPFS Core Operations - add/get/pin
description:
Implement core IPFS operations and expose via RPC endpoints.

## Tasks
1. Implement add() - add content, return CID
2. Implement get() - retrieve content by CID
3. Implement pin() - pin content for persistence
4. Implement unpin() - remove pin
5. Implement listPins() - list all pinned CIDs
6. Create RPC endpoints for all operations
7. Add error handling and validation
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] add() returns valid CID for content
- [ ] get() retrieves exact content by CID
- [ ] pin() successfully pins content
- [ ] unpin() removes pin
- [ ] listPins() returns array of pinned CIDs
- [ ] All RPC endpoints respond correctly
- [ ] Error handling for invalid CIDs
- [ ] Error handling for missing content
close_reason: Completed: Implemented IPFSManager core methods (add/get/pin/unpin/listPins) and RPC endpoints. Commit b7dac5f6.

title: Phase 5: IPFS Public Bridge - Gateway Access
description:
Add optional bridge to public IPFS network for content retrieval and publishing.

## Tasks
1. Configure optional public network connection
2. Implement gateway fetch for public CIDs
3. Add publish to public IPFS option
4. Handle dual-network routing
5. Security considerations for public exposure
design:
### Public Bridge Options
```typescript
interface IPFSManagerConfig {
  privateOnly: boolean;        // Default: true
  publicGateway?: string;      // e.g., https://ipfs.io
  publishToPublic?: boolean;   // Default: false
}
```

### Gateway Methods
```typescript
async fetchFromPublic(cid: string): Promise<Buffer>
async publishToPublic(cid: string): Promise<void>
async isPubliclyAvailable(cid: string): Promise<boolean>
```

### Routing Logic
1. Check private network first
2. If not found and publicGateway configured, try gateway
3. For publish, optionally announce to public DHT

### Security
- Public bridge is opt-in only
- Rate limiting for public fetches
- No automatic public publishing
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Can fetch content from public IPFS gateway
- [ ] Can optionally publish to public IPFS
- [ ] Private network remains default
- [ ] Clear configuration for public access
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents abuse
close_reason: Completed Phase 5 - IPFS Public Bridge implementation with gateway access, publish capability, and rate limiting

title: Phase 6: SDK Integration - sdk.ipfs module (SDK)
description:
Implement sdk.ipfs module in @kynesyslabs/demosdk (../sdks).

⚠️ **SDK ONLY**: All work in ../sdks repository.
After completion, user must manually publish new SDK version.

## Tasks
1. Create IPFS module structure in SDK
2. Implement read methods (demosCall wrappers)
3. Implement transaction builders for writes
4. Add TypeScript types and interfaces
5. Write unit tests
6. Update SDK exports and documentation
7. Publish new SDK version (USER ACTION)
design:
### SDK Structure (../sdks)
```
src/
├── ipfs/
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── types.ts
│   ├── reads.ts      // demosCall wrappers
│   ├── writes.ts     // Transaction builders
│   └── utils.ts
```

### Public Interface
```typescript
class IPFSModule {
  // Reads (demosCall - gas free)
  async get(cid: string): Promise<Buffer>
  async pins(address?: string): Promise<PinInfo[]>
  async status(): Promise<IPFSStatus>
  async rewards(address?: string): Promise<bigint>

  // Writes (Transactions)
  async add(content: Buffer, opts?: AddOptions): Promise<AddResult>
  async pin(cid: string, opts?: PinOptions): Promise<TxResult>
  async unpin(cid: string): Promise<TxResult>
  async claimRewards(): Promise<TxResult>
}
```

### Integration
- Attach to main SDK instance as sdk.ipfs
- Follow existing SDK patterns
- Use shared transaction signing
notes: This phase is SDK-only. User must publish after completion.
close_reason: Phase 6 complete: SDK ipfs module created with IPFSOperations class, payload creators, and utilities. Build verified.

title: Implement async/batched terminal output in CategorizedLogger
description:
Replace synchronous console.log in writeToTerminal with a buffered queue that flushes via setImmediate. This is the highest impact fix for event loop blocking.

Current problem: console.log blocks event loop on every log call (572+ calls in hot paths).

Solution: Buffer terminal output and flush asynchronously using setImmediate or process.nextTick.
close_reason: Implemented async/batched terminal output using setImmediate and process.stdout.write

title: Phase 3: IPFS Streaming - Large Files
description:
Add streaming support for large file uploads and downloads.

## Tasks
1. Implement addStream() for chunked uploads
2. Implement getStream() for streaming downloads
3. Add progress callback support
4. Ensure memory efficiency for large files
5. Update RPC endpoints to support streaming
design:
### Streaming Methods
```typescript
async addStream(
  stream: ReadableStream,
  options?: { filename?: string; onProgress?: (bytes: number) => void }
): Promise<string>

async getStream(
  cid: string,
  options?: { onProgress?: (bytes: number) => void }
): Promise<ReadableStream>
```

### RPC Streaming
- POST /ipfs/add with Transfer-Encoding: chunked
- GET /ipfs/:cid returns streaming response
- Progress via X-Progress header or SSE

### Memory Considerations
- Never load full file into memory
- Use Bun's native streaming capabilities
- Chunk size: 256KB default
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Can upload 1GB+ file without memory issues
- [ ] Can download 1GB+ file without memory issues
- [ ] Progress callbacks fire during transfer
- [ ] RPC endpoints support chunked encoding
- [ ] Memory usage stays bounded during large transfers
close_reason: Implemented IPFS streaming support for large files: addStream() for chunked uploads and getStream() for streaming downloads with progress callbacks. Added RPC endpoints ipfsAddStream and ipfsGetStream with session-based chunk management. Uses 256KB chunks for memory-efficient transfers of 1GB+ files.

description:

- Add tlsnotary module to SDK

- storeProof(tokenId, proof, { storage }): submits TLSN_STORE tx

title: IPFS Integration for Demos Network
description:
Integrate IPFS (Kubo) into Demos nodes with FULL BLOCKCHAIN INTEGRATION for decentralized file storage and P2P content distribution.

## Key Architecture Decisions
- **Reads**: demosCall (gas-free) → ipfs_get, ipfs_pins, ipfs_status
- **Writes**: Demos Transactions (on-chain) → IPFS_ADD, IPFS_PIN, IPFS_UNPIN
- **State**: Account-level ipfs_pins field in StateDB
- **Economics**: Full tokenomics (pay to pin, earn to host)
- **Infrastructure**: Kubo v0.26.0 via Docker Compose (internal network)

## IMPORTANT: SDK Dependency
Transaction types are defined in `../sdks` (@kynesyslabs/demosdk). Each phase involving SDK changes requires:
1. Make changes in ../sdks
2. User manually publishes new SDK version
3. Update SDK version in node package.json
4. Continue with node implementation

## Scope (MVP for Testnet)
- Phase 1: Infrastructure (Kubo Docker + IPFSManager)
- Phase 2: Account State Schema (ipfs_pins field)
- Phase 3: demosCall Handlers (gas-free reads)
- Phase 4: Transaction Types (IPFS_ADD, etc.) - **SDK FIRST**
- Phase 5: Tokenomics (costs + rewards)
- Phase 6: SDK Integration (sdk.ipfs module) - **SDK FIRST**
- Phase 7: Streaming (large files)
- Phase 8: Cluster Sync (private network)
- Phase 9: Public Bridge (optional, lower priority)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kubo container starts with Demos node
- Account state includes ipfs_pins field
- demosCall handlers work for reads
- Transaction types implemented (SDK + Node)
- Tokenomics functional (pay to pin, earn to host)
- SDK sdk.ipfs module works end-to-end
- Large files stream without memory issues
- Private network isolates Demos nodes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Kubo container starts with Demos node
- [ ] Can add content and receive CID
- [ ] Can retrieve content by CID
- [ ] Can pin/unpin content
- [ ] Large files stream without memory issues
- [ ] Private network isolates Demos nodes
- [ ] Optional public IPFS bridge works
close_reason: All 8 phases completed: Phase 1 (Docker + IPFSManager), Phase 2 (Core Operations + Account State), Phase 3 (demosCall Handlers + Streaming), Phase 4 (Transaction Types + Cluster Sync), Phase 5 (Tokenomics + Public Bridge), Phase 6 (SDK Integration), Phase 7 (RPC Handler Integration). All acceptance criteria met: Kubo container integration, add/get/pin operations, large file streaming, private network isolation, and optional public gateway bridge.

title: Convert sync operations to async in log rotation

- getLogsDirSize(): uses fs.readdirSync, fs.statSync

Replace with fs.promises.readdir, fs.promises.stat to prevent blocking every 5 seconds.

title: Integrate IPFS nodes into Docker Compose devnet
description: Add IPFS Kubo containers to the devnet Docker Compose setup so each of the 4 nodes has its own IPFS instance, matching the standalone `./run` script behavior.
design:
## Architecture

Current devnet has:
- 1 shared PostgreSQL (4 databases)
- 4 Demos nodes (node-1 to node-4)

With IPFS integration:
- 1 shared PostgreSQL (unchanged)
- 4 IPFS Kubo containers (ipfs-1 to ipfs-4)
- 4 Demos nodes with IPFS_API_PORT pointing to their IPFS container

## Port Mapping

Following ./run convention (IPFS_API_PORT = PORT + 1000):
- node-1 (53551) → ipfs-1 (54551)
- node-2 (53552) → ipfs-2 (54552)
- node-3 (53553) → ipfs-3 (54553)
- node-4 (53554) → ipfs-4 (54554)

## Environment Variables

Each node needs:
- IPFS_API_PORT: Port of its IPFS container
- IPFS_API_URL: http://ipfs-N:5001 (internal Docker network)

## Volumes

Each IPFS container needs:
- ./ipfs-data/nodeN:/data/ipfs (persistent storage)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] 4 IPFS containers added to docker-compose.yml
- [ ] Each node can reach its IPFS via IPFS_API_PORT
- [ ] IPFS data persists across restarts (optional flag)
- [ ] Health checks work for IPFS containers
- [ ] Documentation updated in devnet/README.md
close_reason: IPFS integration complete: 4 Kubo containers added to devnet with health checks, internal networking, and persistent volumes

title: Phase 4: IPFS Cluster Sync - Private Network
description:
Configure private IPFS network for Demos nodes with cluster pinning.

## Tasks
1. Generate and manage swarm key
2. Configure bootstrap nodes
3. Implement peer discovery using Demos node list
4. Add cluster-wide pinning (pin on multiple nodes)
5. Monitor peer connections
design:
### Swarm Key Management
- Generate key: 64-byte hex string
- Store in config or environment
- Distribute to all Demos nodes

### Bootstrap Configuration
- Remove public bootstrap nodes
- Add Demos bootstrap nodes dynamically
- Use Demos node discovery for peer list

### Cluster Pinning
```typescript
async clusterPin(cid: string, replication?: number): Promise<void>
async getClusterPeers(): Promise<PeerInfo[]>
async connectPeer(multiaddr: string): Promise<void>
```

### Environment Variables
- DEMOS_IPFS_SWARM_KEY
- DEMOS_IPFS_BOOTSTRAP_NODES
- LIBP2P_FORCE_PNET=1
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Swarm key generated and distributed
- [ ] Nodes only connect to other Demos nodes
- [ ] Peer discovery works via Demos network
- [ ] Content replicates across cluster
- [ ] Public IPFS nodes cannot connect
close_reason: Closed via update
